---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
listing: false
---

::: {.hero-shell}
# Mathematical Stories

A constructive route from Euclidean geometry to Cartesian coordinates: not merely how to use axes, but how the coordinate plane is built and why its formulas express pre-existing geometry.
:::

::: {.section-kicker}
Available chapter
:::

<div class="chapter-nav-grid">
<a class="chapter-nav-card" href="chapter-01/index.html"><strong>Chapter 1 — Cartesian Geometry and Coordinates</strong>Build the Cartesian plane from Euclidean constructions, assign coordinates by orthogonal projection, derive distance, and read equations as geometric sets.</a>
<a class="chapter-nav-card" href="chapter-01/constructing-coordinates.html"><strong>Start with the construction</strong>Move step by step from an unlabelled plane to oriented axes with a real numerical scale.</a>
</div>

This site is an HTML edition of the lecture text. Mathematical notation is rendered by MathJax, long reference material is separated from the main narrative, and the original geometric diagrams are recreated as responsive vector graphics.
